list.title: Knowledge Base
list.subtitle: Internal documentation for the team.
list.noArticlesInFolder: No articles in this folder yet — open a sub-folder above to keep browsing.
list.emptyDescription: Capture what your team knows — runbooks, how-tos, onboarding notes. Write your first article or import one from a file.
list.linkedTooltip: {count, plural, one {Linked to # item} other {Linked to # items}}

form.newSubtitle: Write a draft in Markdown — publish it from the article once it's ready.
form.editSubtitle: Changes don't alter the published/draft state — use Publish on the article for that.
form.titlePlaceholder: How to set up the office VPN
form.categoryHint: No categories yet — use the + button to create one.
form.excerptPlaceholder: A one-line summary shown in listings (optional)
form.notSignedInDescription: You must be signed in to edit articles.

toast.signInRequired: You must be signed in to author articles

detail.notFoundDescription: It may be a draft you can't see, it was deleted, or the API is unreachable.
detail.deleteExtra: Published articles are visible to the whole team until then.

links.empty: Not linked to anything yet. Link this article to an asset or application to surface it as that record's runbook.
links.dialogDescription: Link this article to an asset or an application so it shows as that record's runbook. A link points to exactly one target.
links.duplicateHint: Already-linked targets are accepted by the API only once; a duplicate link is rejected.

import.title: Import an article
import.description: Upload a Markdown, plain-text, Word (.docx) or ZIP (.zip) file. Only the text is imported — the original file is not stored.
import.processingHint: Importing your file. Larger files and ZIP archives can take a moment.

toast.signInRequired: You must be signed in to import articles
toast.batchImported: {count, plural, one {# article imported} other {# articles imported}}

batch.title: Import complete
batch.summary: {created, plural, one {# article created} other {# articles created}}{renamed, plural, =0 {} one {, # renamed} other {, # renamed}}{skipped, plural, =0 {} one {, # skipped} other {, # skipped}}
batch.foldersCreated: {count, plural, one {# folder created} other {# folders created}}
batch.linksResolved: {count, plural, one {# wiki-link resolved} other {# wiki-links resolved}}
batch.renamedNote: Slug already taken — auto-suffixed to avoid a conflict.

delete.title: Delete this folder?
delete.warning: {name} and everything inside it will be removed from the Knowledge Base.
delete.subfolderWarning: This also deletes {count, plural, one {# sub-folder} other {# sub-folders}} and all of their articles.
delete.irreversibleNote: Articles in these folders are removed from the KB. This can only be undone by an administrator restoring them in the database.

toast.deleted: Deleted {folders, plural, one {# folder} other {# folders}} and {articles, plural, one {# article} other {# articles}}

references.empty: No other articles reference this one yet. Mention it as a [[slug]] wiki-link from another article to surface it here.

aliases.empty: This article only lives in its home folder. Add it to another folder to surface it there too — an alias never changes its home or who can see it.
aliases.dialogDescription: Place this article in another folder so it also shows up there when browsing. An alias is navigation only — it doesn't move the article's home or change who can see it.

access.publicHint: Visible to all authenticated team members.
access.inheritedRestrictedHint: This folder is restricted because it inherits a restriction from {name}. Add a rule below to narrow access further.
access.restrictedTooltip: Restricted — only specific users may read this folder
